title: Lord Vishnu – The Source of All Creation

analysis.core_story_summary: Driven by the desire, the Adipurush (primitive being) Narayan took the help of his Prakriti (nature's female creational force) and generated himself in Chaturbhuj (four-armed) from. And from His navel appeared Lord Brahma, the creator. That form of God was full of pure truth and from the parts of His body, all the Lokas (worlds) got extension.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Mostly suitable for broad audiences with light cultural context.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Lord Vishnu – The Source of All Creation — Kids (8-12):** Driven by the desire, the Adipurush (primitive being) Narayan took the help of his Prakriti (nature's female creational force) and generated himself in Chaturbhuj (four-armed) from. And from His navel appeared Lord Brahma, the creator. That form of God was full of pure truth and from the parts of His body, all the Lokas (worlds) got extension. This story teaches devotion and disciplined practice, divine grace, truthfulness, respect for nature. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.

And from His navel appeared Lord Brahma, the creator. That form of God was full of pure truth and from the parts of His body, all the Lokas (worlds) got extension. For teens, the key idea is devotion and disciplined practice, divine grace, truthfulness, respect for nature. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.

A modern takeaway is devotion and disciplined practice, divine grace, truthfulness, respect for nature. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Lord Vishnu – The Source of All Creation — Global General Audience:** Driven by the desire, the Adipurush (primitive being) Narayan took the help of his Prakriti (nature's female creational force) and generated himself in Chaturbhuj (four-armed) from. And from His navel appeared Lord Brahma, the creator. That form of God was full of pure truth and from the parts of His body, all the Lokas (worlds) got extension. In universal terms, it explores devotion and disciplined practice, divine grace, truthfulness, respect for nature.
